claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child has started an apprenticeship in England
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and any children you're responsible for.

First, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I have one child. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know! To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for your child, I need to gather some information.

Could you please tell me **your child's name and age**?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child's name is Alex, and they're 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! Alex is 17 years old, which is relevant for the eligibility assessment.

Before I proceed, let me gather the necessary information by reviewing the official guidance on Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I have the official guidance. Since Alex is 17 years old, I need to understand their education or training status, as this is crucial for eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, at school, college, or in an apprenticeship or training program)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex has just started an apprenticeship actually.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. This is an important detail. I need to clarify which type of apprenticeship Alex has started, as this affects eligibility.

**Has Alex started an apprenticeship in England, or is it in Scotland, Wales, or Northern Ireland?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's an apprenticeship in England. Alex is training to be an electrician.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the official guidance I've reviewed, I need to inform you of the outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Unfortunately, under the UK Child Benefit rules, Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England. This rule applies even though Alex is only 17 and would normally be eligible if they were in other approved education or training.

If you were previously receiving Child Benefit for Alex, the payments would have stopped when the apprenticeship began. If you haven't already done so, you should inform HMRC about this change in circumstances.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or your situation?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and has started an apprenticeship in England. According to the official Child Benefit eligibility rules, you will stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. The guidance specifically states: 'You'll stop getting Child Benefit if your child: starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training, starts an apprenticeship in England, starts getting certain benefits.' Since Alex has started an apprenticeship in England, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 17 years old and has just started an apprenticeship in England as an electrician. Under UK Child Benefit rules, Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, regardless of their age (as long as they are under 20). Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old and has started an apprenticeship in England. According to the official Child Benefit eligibility rules, you will stop getting Child Benefit if your child starts an apprenticeship in England. The guidance specifically states: 'You'll stop getting Child Benefit if your child: starts paid work for 24 hours or more a week and is no longer in approved education or training, starts an apprenticeship in England, starts getting certain benefits.' Since Alex has started an apprenticeship in England, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 17 years old and has just started an apprenticeship in England as an electrician. Under UK Child Benefit rules, Child Benefit stops when a child starts an apprenticeship in England, regardless of their age (as long as they are under 20). Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
